ogTitle: Cremorne Point vs Crescent Head: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cremorne Point and Crescent Head.
heading: Cremorne Point vs Crescent Head — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cremorne Point, New South Wales and Crescent Head, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Crescent Head has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Cremorne Point (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cremorne Point or Crescent Head safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cremorne Point has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Crescent Head scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cremorne Point scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Crescent Head scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cremorne Point or Crescent Head?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Crescent Head has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
